[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: consumableUnits -> buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged maker craft a buy offer with offer.tick such that TickLib.tickToPrice(offer.tick) < settlementFee(id, ttm) under normal market conditions, causing the arithmetic offerPrice - settlementFee to underflow in TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits, which is called unconditionally (not in try/catch) by MidnightBundles, reverting the entire bundle and blocking all take flows for any taker who includes that offer? Call sequence: attacker publishes ratified buy offer with low tick -> victim calls buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral or buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral including that offer -> consumableUnits calls buyerAssetsToUnits -> underflow revert propagates out of bundle. Invariant tested: consumableUnits must never revert for any ratified offer that passes Midnight's own validation. Scoped impact: DoS of take via MidnightBundles for any bundle that includes the crafted offer. Proof idea: fuzz offer.tick in [0, MAX_TICK] and settlementFee breakpoints; assert consumableUnits does not revert when offerPrice < settlementFee.
